summary: Re-review after coder v5 (security hardening) and v7 (test gaps). All major findings resolved. Depth limits on all 5 recursive methods, cycle detection, zip bomb tested, Verified locked down, fromJson deduplicated. 1394 tests pass. One new nit (JsonStringNavigator duplication). Approved.

findings.issue: Engine.Types uses non-thread-safe Dictionary<> and HashSet<> for all lookup tables.
findings.resolution: ConcurrentDictionary for mutable collections, lock for _allKinds.

findings.issue: RehydrateNestedData mutates data.Value in-place, clearing _type transiently.
findings.resolution: SetValueDirect() added — updates _value without triggering Value setter side effects.

findings.issue: Decompress used generic Error instead of ServiceError with distinct key.
Compress bypassed Type.Compressible to navigate directly to Engine.Types.

findings.resolution: Acceptable for current dataset. Now uses ConcurrentDictionary.Values.Contains().

findings.issue: Decompress error tests missing Error.Key assertions.
findings.resolution: Error.Key assertions added to all 4 decompress error tests.

findings.issue: GZipDecompress unbounded — no size limit on decompressed output (zip bomb vector).
findings.resolution: MaxDecompressedSize=100MB, chunked read with size check.

findings.issue: Zip bomb size limit (MaxDecompressedSize=100MB) had zero test coverage.
findings.resolution: Decompress_ExceedsSizeLimit_ReturnsError: compresses 110MB of zeros, asserts DecompressError with StatusCode 500 and 'size limit' in message.

findings.issue: Add()/Remove() race window between ConcurrentDictionary write and _allKinds lock.
findings.impact: Transient inconsistency. Nanosecond window. Null return, not corruption.
findings.suggestion: Accept as benign. Kind-as-rich-object refactor would solve naturally.

findings.issue: UnwrapElement/UnwrapObject/UnwrapArray duplicate Data.UnwrapJsonElement logic. Same recursive JSON unwrapping in two places. Not a security risk (bounded by STJ MaxDepth=64) but a maintenance risk — fixes must be applied in two places.
findings.impact: Low. Bug fixes to JSON unwrapping could miss one of the two implementations.
findings.suggestion: Have JsonStringNavigator call Data.UnwrapJsonElement (internal static, same assembly). Align number handling (int vs long for small integers).
